description_long: This is a small, single-propeller airplane with a streamlined fuselage painted in a vibrant green color. The wings are straight and slightly angled, attached at a mid-level position on the fuselage, and extend outward with no visible winglets. The cockpit is enclosed with a blue-tinted, multi-panel canopy situated towards the front of the fuselage, suggesting a single or dual-seat configuration. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all painted green. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the landing gear is not visible, implying it may be retractable or omitted in this representation. The overall design is simple and functional, suitable for light aviation or training purposes.
description: A small, green, single-propeller airplane with a blue-tinted enclosed cockpit and conventional tail. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, and the design is streamlined and functional.
description_view_0: Front view showing the nose-mounted propeller, blue-tinted cockpit canopy, and the full wingspan.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, mid-mounted straight wing, and the cockpit's position near the front.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, as well as the back of the wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same features as view 1, with clear visibility of the propeller and tail structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a blue-tinted canopy, likely single or dual-seat
